title: astragalus newberryi var. aquarii
intro: Astragalus newberryi var. aquarii, also known as Newberry's milkvetch, is a perennial plant belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae) and is native to the southwestern United States. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its adaptogenic and immunomodulatory properties, which can help to boost the body's natural defenses and improve its ability to cope with stress. The medicinal properties of Astragalus newberryi var. aquarii are attributed to its high content of saponins and flavonoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, providing various health benefits such as improved cardiovascular health, enhanced immune function, and reduced fatigue. Astragalus newberryi var. aquarii can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often used in combination with other herbal remedies to enhance its therapeutic effects.
